title: [Record] Stride-32 + Warmdown/Muon Tuning on SOTA #1: mean val_bpb=1.1403
body:
## Summary

  Stride-32 sliding window eval + warmdown/momentum tuning on the 10L Int5MLP BigramHash SWA
  base. Mean val_bpb = 1.1403 across 3 seeds.

  ## 3-Seed Results

  | Seed | val_loss (nats) | val_bpb | artifact_bytes |
  |------|----------------|---------|---------------|
  | 42 | 1.92354186 | 1.13923361 | 15,817,986 |
  | 1337 | 1.92776687 | 1.14173591 | 15,676,104 |
  | 7 | 1.92484360 | 1.14000458 | 15,723,922 |
  | **Mean** | **1.92538** | **1.14032** | |

  Improvement over SOTA #1 (1.1428 BPB / 1.942 nats): **-0.017 nats** (3.4x the 0.005 threshold).

  ## Changes from SOTA #1

  Training (env var overrides only — unmodified train_gpt.py):
  - `EVAL_STRIDE=32` (from 64): 2x context overlap at eval
  - `WARMDOWN_ITERS=5000` (from 3000): extended warmdown
  - `MUON_MOMENTUM=0.95` (from 0.99): faster gradient adaptation
  - `TRAIN_BATCH_TOKENS=524288` (from 786432): more gradient steps

  All artifacts under 16MB (int6+zstd-22). All runs under 600s on 8xH100 SXM.

  Note: The record submission is in
  `records/track_10min_16mb/2026-03-20_Stride32_WarmdownTuned_MuonTuned/`. Other folders in this
  PR are from earlier non-record iterations.

  Hardware: 8x NVIDIA H100 80GB HBM3 (SXM), RunPod.
